title: Second Variety and Other Classic Stories
author: Philip K. Dick
card_summary: A collection of Philip K. Dick's classic short stories, anchored by the title story "Second Variety." At 499 pages, the volume includes major stories such as The Cookie Lady, Beyond the Door, Jon's World, The Commuter, Breakfast at Twilight, The Hood Maker, Human Is, Adjustment Team, The Impossible Planet, Impostor, James P. Crow, Planet for Transients, Small Town, and Prominent Author, among many others. Represents Dick's extraordinary range and productivity in the short fiction form.
page_summary:
Second Variety and Other Classic Stories is a comprehensive collection of Philip K. Dick's short fiction, anchored by the title story "Second Variety," one of Dick's most celebrated early works about self-replicating war machines. At 499 pages, this is a substantial volume spanning much of Dick's career in the short fiction form.

The table of contents reveals an extraordinary range of stories: The Cookie Lady, Beyond the Door, Second Variety, Jon's World, The Cosmic Poachers, Progeny, Some Kinds of Life, Martians Come in Clouds, The Commuter, The World She Wanted, A Surface Raid, Project: Earth, The Trouble with Bubbles, Breakfast at Twilight, A Present for Pat, The Hood Maker, Of Withered Apples, Human Is, Adjustment Team, The Impossible Planet, Impostor, James P. Crow, Planet for Transients, Small Town, Souvenir, Survey Team, and Prominent Author, with notes.

Many of these stories have been adapted into films or television episodes. "Second Variety" became the film Screamers (1995), "Impostor" became a 2001 film, and "Adjustment Team" was adapted as The Adjustment Bureau (2011). The collection showcases Dick's mastery of the short story form and his ability to pack profound philosophical questions about identity, reality, and humanity into compact narrative structures.
